title: Register as a resident with a Canadian business - Canada.ca
content:
## Register as a resident with a Canadian business
You may be looking for: Register as a non-resident

## Canada Post in labour negotiations – strike paused
Canada Post operations have resumed. Tentative agreements have been reached, and Canada Post and its union have agreed not to engage in any strike or lockout activity until the ratification process is complete. For more information on the impact on CRA services, visit our postal interruption page .

## Business registration by phone is no longer available
Effective November 3, 2025 , the CRA will no longer accept business number (BN) or CRA program account registrations by phone. You must register online using Business Registration Online (BRO) .
Registering online is the fastest and easiest way to get a business number (BN) and certain CRA program accounts such as GST/HST and payroll.
- Register with Business Registration Online (BRO)
- GST/HST for a business in Quebec
- Program accounts with a separate registration process

## Register with Business Registration Online (BRO)
You may already have a BN if you have registered with the CRA before, incorporated federally, or registered or incorporated your business in certain provinces or territories. A BN may also have been created if you started a registration in BRO but your session timed out.
To find out which provinces and territories issue a BN when you register or incorporate, go to When you get a BN as part of other registrations .
If you do not know your BN , go to Find an existing business number .
You can register for any of the following at the same time:
- Business number (BN)
- GST/HST (RT) excluding Quebec
- Payroll deductions (RP)
- Corporation income tax (RC)
- Registered charity (RR)
- Information returns (RZ)
- Underused Housing Tax (RU)
- Global minimum tax (PT)
You cannot complete the following using BRO :
- Reactivate a previously closed program account
- Register a business owned by another business, for example, where the partner is a corporation or partnership
- Register a business when the owner or director is deceased, for example, when an estate needs to file business taxes
- Register a Canadian business with only non-resident owners

### Important information for individuals with a temporary SIN starting with 9
Individuals with a temporary SIN that starts with 9 must now use BRO to register for a BN and most CRA program accounts.
- Sole proprietors with a temporary SIN starting with 9 must now use BRO to register for a GST/HST program account
- Directors, partners, and trustees with a temporary SIN beginning with 9 cannot use BRO to register for a GST/HST program account. You must use Form RC1 , Request for a Business Number and Certain Program Accounts , instead
Things to keep in mind:
- You must save or print your BN for your records, it won't be sent to you
- Your online session times out after 10 minutes of inactivity
- You cannot save information during the registration process
- You can access the Ontario and Nova Scotia registration sites directly from BRO Residents of Ontario and Nova Scotia After registering for a BN and CRA program accounts in BRO , you can link directly to the provincial registration site. Your BRO session will end once you select Transfer to provincial site .
After registering for a BN and CRA program accounts in BRO , you can link directly to the provincial registration site. Your BRO session will end once you select Transfer to provincial site .
Available daily except between 3 am to 6 am ET for system maintenance
If you try to enter your address in BRO and your postal code is not accepted, complete your business number and CRA program account registration using Form RC1 , Request for a Business Number and Certain Program Accounts .
Access BRO through one of the following options:
For business owners:
- Select Add account , then Business account
- Select Register a business
For representatives with a RepID, you can access BRO through Represent a Client.
You must provide additional personal information to verify your identity.
The person submitting the registration must provide their:
- Social insurance number (SIN)
- Postal code of their home address

### If you are unable to register online
Visit our postal interruption page for details on how CRA services are impacted by the Canada Post strike. We are monitoring the situation and will provide updates as needed.

#### Phone registration is no longer available
Effective November 3, 2025 , the CRA will no longer accept business number (BN) or CRA program account registrations by phone.
If you cannot complete your registration online using BRO , you can:
You can register for a BN and the following CRA program accounts on one form:
- Payroll deductions (RP)
- Corporation income tax (RC)
- Registered charity (RR)
- Information returns (RZ)
You cannot use this form to register for the following:
- Underused Housing Tax (RU)
- Fill out the appropriate sections you want to register for on Form RC1 , Request for a Business Number and Certain Program Accounts
- Send your completed form to your tax centre
If you are unable to register online for the Luxury Tax (LT) using BRO , you can fill out the required LT registration forms for your business and send them:
- Electronically using "Submit documents" in your CRA account for business
- By mail to the address on the last page of the form (based on the location of your business)
For registration details: Luxury Tax registration

## GST/HST for a business in Quebec
If your business is physically located in Quebec, the GST/HST is administered by Revenu Québec. You must register and file your GST/HST returns with Revenu Québec and use their forms.
For more information about the GST/HST for Quebec businesses:
- Revenu Québec – General information concerning the QST and the GST/HST
- Contact Revenu Québec – General information for businesses

## Program accounts with a separate registration process
You can only register for certain program accounts using Business Registration Online (BRO) . If the program account you need is not available in BRO , you may still use BRO to register for a BN .

### How to register for these program accounts
Find out how to register for:
The only option to register is to fill in a paper form.
If you do not have a BN : You must get a BN before you register for this program account.
For registration details: Air Travellers Security Charge program account
The only option to register is through the Digital services tax account registration web form.
If you do not have a BN : You will get a BN through the registration process.
For registration details: How to register for a Digital services tax (DST) program account
The only option to register is to fill out the required forms related to your business product.
If you do not have a BN : You must get a BN before you register for this program account.
For registration details: Excise duty forms
The only option to register is to fill out the required forms related to your business.
If you do not have a BN : You must get a BN before you register for this program account.
For registration details: Excise taxes and other levies
As of October 21, 2024 , the import and export program account is administered by the Canada Border Service Agency (CBSA) .
If you do not have a BN : Depending on your business type, you can get a BN either through the CBSA Assessment and Revenue Management system (CARM) or BRO .
For registration details: Register for or modify an import-export program account
The only option to register is to fill out a form.
If you do not have a BN : You must get a BN before you register for this program account.
For registration details: Insurance premium tax program account

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
